project.name: Digital Avatar Agent System
project.branch: v14 -> additive path toward avatar/agent architecture
project.mission: Scale a founder's decision pattern across specialized agents without sacrificing current function.

additive_redesign: No redesign may remove an already-working function without a drop-in replacement that preserves current behavior.
New modules enter behind flags, adapters, or side paths first.
Current v14 stage remains the executable baseline until the new path matches or exceeds it on agreed checks.
State shape changes require compatibility loaders or migration scripts.
Every major redesign must prove non-regression on trace, corridor, readout, and state export.
design_bias: Prefer more configs before more engine surgery.
Separate execution substrate from reasoning substrate.

confirmed_from_current_files: Current entrypoint seeds a signed field, instantiates SignatureState, runs the reasoning loop, rebuilds the dynamic corridor, applies binary readout, and exports a structured state JSON.
The uploaded chat file is still a placeholder, so the current build is engine-first rather than interface-first.

layers.name: L0 core state engine
layers.purpose: signed field, amplitude, phase, stability, residue, trace

layers.name: L1 conditioning engine
layers.purpose: scenario loops that write initial residue and corridor tendencies

layers.name: L3 role overlays
layers.purpose: sales/ops/strategy specializations without breaking core identity

layers.name: L4 agent runtime
layers.purpose: spawn, task, observe, re-align, retire agents

layers.name: L5 orchestration
layers.purpose: task router, arbitration, shared memory exchange, dashboards

phases.phase: Phase 0 - Freeze and instrument v14 baseline
phases.goal: Make the present engine the regression anchor.
deliverables: baseline config pack: low/mid/high corridor, stress, signed-init, amplitude-compat
non-regression harness for output/confidence/trace shape/state schema

phases.phase: Phase 1 - Add explicit residue and conditioning loop
phases.goal: Move from raw response engine to shaped developmental engine.
deliverables: residue tensor or equivalent state channel
conditioning runner with bounded scenario episodes
scenario library v1 (risk, ambiguity, conflict, repetition, delay, ethical tension)
metrics: stability, admissibility retention, write/decay balance, replay sensitivity

phases.goal: Create a founder-aligned snapshot that can be validated.
deliverables: comparison report: founder vs avatar choices on novel prompts

phases.goal: Fork controlled continuations that keep the core but specialize by role.
Turn aligned agents into a usable operating system for work.

phases.phase: Phase 5 - Optimize substrate without losing function
phases.goal: Port stable abstractions toward AVX2/analog substrate after software behavior is pinned down.
deliverables: compatibility suite between Python reference and accelerated backend

first_week_plan: Freeze current v14 as baseline_ref/ and do not mutate it directly.
Add a baseline regression script around the current stage1 entrypoint.
Define v14 state schema in one JSON document.
Add explicit residue channel in a branch behind config flag use_residue_v1=false by default.
Create five conditioning scenario templates and one runner that writes reports only.
Add identity capture JSON format for founder preferences, tradeoffs, and refusal patterns.
Stub the chat shell around the engine instead of building a full UI.

phase_0: Current outputs reproducible from fixed seed/config
phase_1: Conditioning loop changes residue in measurable and repeatable ways
No regression in baseline path when conditioning disabled
phase_2: Avatar aligns with founder decisions on a held-out scenario pack above chosen threshold
phase_3: At least three role agents diverge in specialization while staying within alignment band

immediate_notes: The current uploaded stage1 script already gives a good seam for this work because it centralizes initialization, config loading, reasoning execution, corridor reconstruction, readout, and export.
The current chat file should stay lightweight until identity validation is real; interface follows engine, not the reverse.
Additive redesign means the v14 baseline remains runnable throughout.
